summary: Matched most of Popo's side-B callback set in ftPp_SpecialS.c, replacing many m2c stubs with C for animation, IASA, physics, collision, accessory/item cleanup, and effect/JObj update callbacks. The PR also refined Popo/Ice Climber types and attributes needed by the matches, exposed climbers-string item functions, adjusted ftCommon_8007D2E8's signature, and moved the relevant .data range from Nana init into Popo side-B's split. decomp.dev reported 17 new full matches worth +5092 bytes and one near-match for ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 at 98.86%. No human review feedback was present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented ftPp_SpecialS_8011F720, which updates the side-B related GObj/JObj visibility and rotation, hides or shows a child JObj depending on ftPp_MS_SpecialS1 or ftPp_MS_SpecialS2, rotates it from the floor normal with atan2f when cmd_vars[3] and mv.pp.specials.xC are set, and installs efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.

Implemented side-B air animation callbacks ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Anim and ftPp_SpecialAirS2_Anim, including cleanup of take_dmg_cb, death2_cb, pre_hitlag_cb, post_hitlag_cb, and x1A5C before entering Fall or FallSpecial-style behavior depending on Ice Climber attribute x70.

Implemented ground and air IASA callbacks for S1/S2. Ground versions use attribute x30 for stick-driven mv.pp.specials.x1C, air versions use x34, and S2 variants delegate to the corresponding S1 helper.

Implemented ground physics callbacks ftPp_SpecialS1_Phys and ftPp_SpecialS2_Phys, including ground acceleration/friction handling, slope-normal contribution via attribute x6C, B-button command timing through mv.pp.specials.x10/x14, transition to ftPp_MS_SpecialAirS1 or ftPp_MS_SpecialAirS2, and vertical boosts from attributes x60 and x64.

Implemented air physics callbacks ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Phys and ftPp_SpecialAirS2_Phys, including B-button boost timing, velocity decay/acceleration via ftCommon_8007D494 or ftCommon_8007D4B8, horizontal control via ftCommon_8007D2E8, and separate attribute slots for S1/S2 air behavior.

Implemented ground and air collision callbacks for SpecialS1/S2. These use a local ftCollisionBox at 0x803CD820, ft_80082888 for ground collision tests, ft_800824A0 for air-to-ground transitions, wall-bounce behavior using env_flags and attributes x44/x48, and shared motion transition helpers for ground-air state changes.

Added Popo side-B utility functions ftPp_SpecialS_801210C8, ftPp_SpecialS_8012114C, and ftPp_SpecialS_80121164 for climbers-string item creation, held item bookkeeping, and cleanup/destruction through it_802C27D4 and it_802C2750.

Partially matched ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, which computes a normalized velocity vector based on player/Nana-related positioning and scales it with Ice Climber attributes x94 and x98; decomp.dev reported it at 98.86%, not a full match.
Updated ftPp_SpecialS.h to expose ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0, ftPp_SpecialS_801210C8, and ftPp_SpecialS_8012114C.

Refined ftPopo/types.h: fv.pp.x2238 became an Item_GObj pointer, mv.pp.specials.x8 became a small struct pointer containing an HSD_GObj pointer at x4, mv.pp.specials.x18 became an int, and many ftIceClimberAttributes offsets from x30 through x98 were identified as floats/ints rather than padding.
Changed ftCommon_8007D2E8 from a three-argument declaration/definition to a four-argument signature and updated ftCommon_8007D3A8 to pass its fourth argument through.
Added climbers-string item declarations it_802C2750 and it_802C27D4 to itclimbersstring.h.
Adjusted GALE01 splits so .data 0x803CD7F8-0x803CD838 belongs to ftPp_SpecialS.c, with Nana init data starting at 0x803CD838, and marked ftNn_Init_803CD820 as data:float in symbols.txt.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers to centralize callback cleanup, part reset/ref-GObj cleanup, slope rotation, and ground/air motion transitions, which reduced duplication while preserving the shape needed for matching.

Reused S1 implementations for identical S2 IASA behavior and kept separate physics/collision functions where attribute offsets or motion IDs differ, a useful balance between readable decomp and exact-code-shape constraints.

Refined previously opaque fighter vars before using them: x2238 as Item_GObj*, mv.pp.specials.x8 as a nested struct with an HSD_GObj* at x4, and x18 as an int. This made the callback implementations express real object relationships instead of raw offsets.

Matched side-B visual/effect behavior by explicitly pairing pre_hitlag_cb/post_hitlag_cb with efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll and by clearing those callbacks when the side-B object state becomes invalid.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK, temporary variable structure, explicit casts, and separate local temporaries in larger physics/collision callbacks.
Moved the relevant data split rather than leaving Popo side-B data attributed to Nana init, making the source-unit layout line up with the newly implemented static data and callbacks.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter special references partner/items/effects, matching often requires typing the motion vars and fighter vars first; otherwise callback code stays stuck as raw pointer arithmetic.
Ground and air variants of a move can often be matched by identifying which attribute slots differ, then sharing only the callbacks whose assembly is truly identical.
Collision callbacks for sliding moves need careful env_flags side selection: positive velocity checks low wall bits like 0x3F, while negative velocity checks the opposite mask such as 0xFC0.

Slope-aware visual alignment appears in both part rotation and attached JObj rotation; the PR used atan2f of floor.normal.x/floor.normal.y with facing/sign differences depending on the object being rotated.

Side-effect callbacks such as take_dmg_cb, death2_cb, death3_cb, pre_hitlag_cb, post_hitlag_cb, and x1A5C are part of the match, not just gameplay cleanup. Leaving stale callbacks can also prevent later functions from matching cleanly.
Data ownership in splits.txt may need to move as a file becomes decompiled. Here, the .data range before Nana's motion state table was reassigned to ftPp_SpecialS.c.
A near-match should be recorded explicitly. ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 improved to 98.86% but was not fully matched according to the bot report.
naming_conventions: Popo side-B callbacks follow ftPp_SpecialS*, ftPp_SpecialAirS*, and suffixes _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, _Coll.
Motion IDs use ftPp_MS_SpecialS1, ftPp_MS_SpecialS2, ftPp_MS_SpecialAirS1, and ftPp_MS_SpecialAirS2.
Unknown but address-stable helpers remain address-named, such as ftPp_SpecialS_8011F720, ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, ftPp_SpecialS_801210C8, ftPp_SpecialS_8012114C, and ftPp_SpecialS_80121164.
Newly identified Popo fighter vars kept offset names while improving types, for example fv.pp.x2238 as Item_GObj*.
The Ice Climber attributes struct kept offset-style names x30, x34, x38, x3C, x40, x44, x48, x4C, x50, x54, x58, x5C, x60, x64, x6C, x70, x94, and x98 because semantic names were not evidenced.
The local static collision box retained the address-derived name ftNn_Init_803CD820 despite being moved into ftPp_SpecialS.c data ownership.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in callbacks with stack-sensitive matching, including 0x18, 0x20, and 8-byte padding cases.
Kept some non-ideal temporary naming and repeated GET_FIGHTER calls to preserve compiler output in large physics/collision callbacks.
Used inline helper functions rather than normal calls where code needed to be shared without changing generated assembly call structure.
Added a fourth parameter to ftCommon_8007D2E8 and propagated it through ftCommon_8007D3A8 so Popo air physics could call ftCommon_8007D2E8(fp, speed, cap, 0.0F) with the expected signature.
Declared item functions in itclimbersstring.h so ftPp_SpecialS_801210C8 and ftPp_SpecialS_80121164 could express climbers-string spawn/destroy behavior directly.
Used a local static ftCollisionBox data object and adjusted splits/symbol metadata so data layout would match the binary.
Used math helpers atan2f, sqrtf, SQ, and lbVector_Normalize to express slope rotation and partner-relative velocity logic in C.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 45.26% (+0.13%, +5092 bytes).

decomp.dev reported 17 new full matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS: ftPp_SpecialS_8011F720, ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Coll, ftPp_SpecialAirS2_Coll, ftPp_SpecialS1_Coll, ftPp_SpecialS2_Coll, ftPp_SpecialS1_Phys, ftPp_SpecialS2_Phys, ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Phys, ftPp_SpecialAirS2_Phys, ftPp_SpecialAirS2_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Anim, ftPp_SpecialS_801210C8, ftPp_SpecialS1_IASA, ftPp_SpecialS2_IASA, ftPp_SpecialAirS1_IASA, ftPp_SpecialAirS2_IASA, and ftPp_SpecialS_80121164.
decomp.dev reported ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 improved from 0.00% to 98.86%, so it remained an unmatched or not-perfectly-matched function in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that finish matching ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 from 98.86% to 100%.
Look for later renames of ftIceClimberAttributes x30-x98 fields once their side-B semantics are understood.
Search for later cleanup of the address-derived static data name ftNn_Init_803CD820 after the Popo side-B data split change.
Review other Ice Climber item callbacks using fv.pp.x2238 and itclimbersstring to confirm consistent Item_GObj typing.
Search for other ftCommon_8007D2E8 call sites affected by the fourth-argument signature.
